# Agentic Flow v2.5.2 - run log

**Date:** 2026-04-20
**Branch:** `v2.5.2`
**Scope:** Planned architecture and workflow stabilization from v2.5.1 analysis
**Compared to:** `v2.5.1`

---

## Version Updates

### Planned Actions

This file is created as the pre-run planning log for `v2.5.2`.

`v2.5.2` is intentionally a stabilization increment, not a new functional step. The focus is to reduce architecture friction, context waste, prompt drift, and verification ambiguity before attempting broader flow changes.

| Priority | Severity | Planned action | Effort | Why it matters | Expected impact | Target files / folders | Dependencies |
|----------|----------|----------------|--------|----------------|-----------------|------------------------|--------------|
| P1 | HIGH | Redesign the plan around slices and affected classes, while keeping external client and GitLab contracts in the story `External Contracts` section | Large | This is the biggest root cause behind Architect authoring pain and Coder context waste | Major reduction in plan size, easier revision, cleaner review targeting | `flow-log/schema/plan-v3.schema.json`, `flow-log/src/plan/**`, `flow-log/docs/plan-management.md`, `.github/agents/java-architect.agent.md`, `artifacts/templates/user-story.md` | None |
| P1 | HIGH | Add active slice IDs to `summary` and support `plan-get --slice <id>` or equivalent targeted retrieval | Medium | Coder still reads too much and TL still passes too little | Major reduction in coder context load and faster batches | `flow-log/src/log/summary.mjs`, `flow-log/src/log/batches.mjs`, `flow-log/src/plan-commands/query.mjs`, `.github/agents/team-lead.agent.md`, `.github/agents/java-coder.agent.md`, `.github/agents/reviewer.agent.md`, `.github/agents/architecture-reviewer.agent.md` | Benefits from the slice-first plan redesign |
| P1 | HIGH | Make stale `finalCheck` and `karate` block `readiness signoff` | Small | Current signoff can declare ready while evidence is stale | Stronger verification integrity and less silent drift | `flow-log/src/log/gates.mjs`, `flow-log/src/log/summary.mjs`, `flow-log/README.md`, `.github/agentic-flow/agentic-flow-overview.md` | None |
| P1 | MEDIUM | Remove TL default per-batch `finalCheck` reruns and trust `batch-verify` unless evidence is stale or suspect | Small | Current verification flow still burns time with low added value | Faster implementation loop and less redundant verification | `.github/agents/team-lead.agent.md`, `.github/agentic-flow/agentic-flow-overview.md`, `.github/instructions/local-quality-rules.instructions.md` | None |
| P2 | HIGH | Enforce `--plan-ref` in CLI/docs, not only in prompts | Small | Current rule is still inconsistent across prompts, docs, and CLI help | Cleaner surgical revision loop and less drift | `flow-log/src/log-commands/risks.mjs`, `flow-log/src/log-commands/index.mjs`, `flow-log/docs/review-commands.md`, `flow-log/test/**` | None |
| P2 | MEDIUM | Add a repo-root-safe `flow-log` wrapper or canonical command path | Medium | Prompt reminders alone are not preventing the wrong-folder failure | Removes a recurring failure mode and reduces recovery time | `scripts/**`, `flow-log/README.md`, `.github/agents/java-coder.agent.md`, `.github/agents/team-lead.agent.md`, `.github/agents/reviewer.agent.md`, `.github/agents/architecture-reviewer.agent.md` | None |
| P2 | MEDIUM | Remove documentation work from Architect plan payload and assign one clear owner | Medium | Architect and TL still carry too much non-core work; plans still embed documentation tasks despite prompt cleanup | Smaller plans and cleaner role focus | `.github/agents/java-architect.agent.md`, `.github/agents/reviewer.agent.md`, `.github/agentic-flow/agentic-flow-overview.md`, `flow-log/docs/plan-management.md` | Best done together with the plan redesign |
| P2 | MEDIUM | Resolve prompt / doc drift and add a lightweight drift check | Small-Medium | Contradictory instructions still exist and confuse edge-case behavior | More reliable agent execution and fewer prompt regressions | `.github/agents/code-review-refactorer.agent.md`, `flow-log/docs/review-commands.md`, `.github/agentic-flow/agentic-flow-overview.md`, optional drift-check script under `scripts/` | None |
| P3 | MEDIUM | Test Coder on GPT-5.4 or Auto only after slice-scoped context changes land | Small | Model swap may help, but today it would be confounded by the bigger context problem | Possible further speed and reasoning gain | `.github/agents/java-coder.agent.md`, `v2.5.2.md` version notes | Depends on the P1 slice-context work |

### Findings Targeted From v2.5.1 Analysis

| Issue spotted in v2.5.1 | Planned response in v2.5.2 | Severity | Effort | Expected pain relief |
|-------------------------|-------------------------------|----------|--------|----------------------|
| Plan still too large and too hard for Architect to author and revise | Replace section-heavy plan design with a slice-first structure centered on affected classes, light note fields, and story-owned `External Contracts` | HIGH | Large | Shorter plans, fewer draft rewrites, easier architectural review |
| Coder still defaults to full-plan reads and TL passes only feature name | Add slice-aware summary output and slice-scoped plan retrieval; include active slice IDs in TL handoff | HIGH | Medium | Less context waste and faster implementation batches |
| `readiness signoff` ignores stale PASS evidence | Make stale `finalCheck` and `karate` fail signoff readiness | HIGH | Small | Better evidence integrity and fewer false-ready states |
| TL still reruns expensive checks after green coder batch verification | Make batch-level TL rechecks conditional on stale or suspect evidence only | MEDIUM | Small | Lower implementation cost with little quality risk |
| Wrong-directory `flow-log` failure still happens in practice | Add tooling-level root-safe invocation instead of relying on prompt discipline only | MEDIUM | Medium | Fewer terminal failures and less recovery time |
| Docs ownership still bleeds into Architect scope | Remove docs from Architect-owned plan content and keep one explicit owner | MEDIUM | Medium | Cleaner role focus and smaller plans |
| Prompt/doc/CLI rule drift remains | Clean up contradictions and add a lightweight drift-check step | MEDIUM | Small-Medium | More predictable agent behavior |
| Coder may need a stronger model, but evidence is confounded by current context waste | Defer the model experiment until after the handoff/context fixes | MEDIUM | Small | Cleaner experiment with better signal |

### Slice-Based Plan Design Proposal

This is the validated proposal for the **slice-first plan redesign** targeted by `v2.5.2`.

#### Recommended Direction

Keep the **tool-owned draft lifecycle**.

Do **not** switch to Markdown authoring for `v2.5.2`.

Reasoning:

- The current draft lifecycle already gives us the right control points: canonical artifact, draft editing, validation, approval invalidation, and deterministic state transitions.
- The main problem is not "JSON exists". The main problem is that the current `v3` JSON shape is too section-heavy and duplicates information across top-level inventories and slice references.
- Introducing Markdown plus a translation step would add a second failure mode: now the system would need to validate both the authored Markdown structure and the generated JSON. That is too much moving surface for a stabilization iteration.
- A **leaner machine-readable schema** gives the same targeted retrieval benefits we want for Coder, Reviewer, and TL without adding a second authoring format.

Recommendation: keep `plan-init-draft` / `plan-create-draft` / `plan-validate-draft` / `plan-accept-draft`, but replace the current plan shape with a new **slice-first `schemaVersion: "4.0"`**.

`4.0` is recommended rather than `3.x` because the change is structurally breaking: top-level inventories stop being the primary authoring surface, external contracts move out of the plan into the story `External Contracts` section, and retrieval semantics change from section-driven to slice-driven.

#### Proposed `schemaVersion: "4.0"` Structure

Exact proposed top-level structure:

```json
{
  "schemaVersion": "4.0",
  "feature": "milestones-search-api",
  "revision": 1,
  "status": "draft",
  "scope": {
    "purpose": "...",
    "inScope": ["..."],
    "outOfScope": ["..."],
    "constraints": ["..."]
  },
  "sharedRules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "rule": "Reuse the existing validation error contract.",
      "appliesTo": ["S1", "S2"]
    }
  ],
  "sharedNotes": [
    "Keep project identity server-owned.",
    "If a slice touches external payloads, see story.md -> External Contracts instead of duplicating payloads here."
  ],
  "slices": [
    {
      "id": "S1",
      "title": "Milestones REST contract",
      "goal": "Add the REST boundary, orchestration input, and response mapping for milestone search.",
      "dependsOn": [],
      "sliceNotes": [
        "This slice owns request defaults and response shape.",
        "See story.md -> External Contracts for client request/response examples."
      ],
      "readsExisting": [
        "common/web/GlobalExceptionHandler.java"
      ],
      "affectedClasses": [
        {
          "id": "S1-A1",
          "module": "flow-orchestrator",
          "package": "orchestration/milestones/rest",
          "class": "MilestonesController",
          "status": "new",
          "purpose": "REST entry point for milestone search.",
          "change": "Add POST endpoint, allow omitted body, map request to service, and return the response DTO.",
          "tests": {
            "levels": ["integration"],
            "notes": "Cover omitted body, happy path, invalid state, duplicate ids, and malformed JSON."
          },
          "loggingNotes": "Log effective filters and result count without raw payload values."
        },
        {
          "id": "S1-A2",
          "module": "flow-orchestrator",
          "package": "orchestration/milestones",
          "class": "SearchMilestonesInput",
          "status": "new",
          "purpose": "Orchestration input for milestone search.",
          "change": "Add defaulting and immutable list handling for search inputs before the service calls the port.",
          "tests": {
            "levels": ["unit"],
            "notes": "Cover defaulting, list immutability, and empty input behavior."
          },
          "loggingNotes": "No direct logging; logging stays in controller and service."
        }
      ],
      "doneWhen": [
        "The endpoint exists and the optional body defaults are preserved.",
        "Validation behavior matches the locked request contract.",
        "The slice passes its planned unit and integration coverage."
      ]
    }
  ],
  "finalVerification": {
    "requiredGates": ["verifyQuick", "finalCheck", "karate"],
    "notes": [
      "finalCheck and karate must be fresh at signoff."
    ]
  },
  "hash": null
}
```

#### Required Vs Optional Fields

Required top-level fields:

- `schemaVersion`
- `feature`
- `revision`
- `status`
- `scope`
- `slices`
- `finalVerification`

Optional top-level fields:

- `sharedRules`
- `sharedNotes`
- `hash` (tool-maintained)

Required `scope` fields:

- `purpose`
- `inScope`
- `outOfScope`

Optional `scope` fields:

- `constraints`

Required `sharedRules[]` fields when that section is present:

- `id`
- `rule`
- `appliesTo`

Optional slice fields:

- `sliceNotes`
- `readsExisting`

Required slice fields:

- `id`
- `title`
- `goal`
- `dependsOn`
- `affectedClasses`
- `doneWhen`

Required `affectedClasses[]` fields:

- `id`
- `module`
- `package`
- `class`
- `status`
- `purpose`
- `change`
- `tests`

Optional `affectedClasses[]` fields:

- `loggingNotes`
- `pathHint` when `module` + `package` + `class` is not enough to disambiguate the file

Required `tests` fields:

- `levels`
- `notes`

Required `finalVerification` fields:

- `requiredGates`

Optional `finalVerification` fields:

- `notes`

#### Why This Shape Is Better Than `v3`

- The **slice** becomes the primary planning unit, which matches how the feature is implemented and reviewed.
- `affectedClasses` carry enough implementation direction without turning the plan into a pseudo-code schema.
- `module` + `package` + `class` is cleaner for agents than full filesystem paths; full paths stay optional only when ambiguity exists.
- `readsExisting` becomes a grep clue, not an architectural object.
- `sharedRules` are explicitly linkable through ids, while `sharedNotes` and `sliceNotes` stay lightweight and unstructured.
- External client and GitLab contracts live once in story.md -> `External Contracts` instead of being duplicated inside the plan.

#### Creation Flow

Recommended creation path:

1. Keep `plan-init-draft` as the preferred Architect entry point.
2. Change the initial skeleton to the `4.0` shape above: `scope`, `sharedRules`, `sharedNotes`, `slices`, `finalVerification`, `hash`.
3. PM seeds story.md -> `External Contracts` when the feature touches external request/response or GitLab API boundaries.
4. Architect validates and updates story.md -> `External Contracts` from official docs when needed, then fills the plan in this order:
   - `scope`
  - `sharedRules` (only truly cross-slice rules)
  - `sharedNotes` only when they add useful context
   - `slices[]` one by one
   - `finalVerification`
5. `plan-validate-draft` runs schema, readiness, and risk-link validation.
6. If the Architect changed story.md -> `External Contracts`, TL re-registers and re-approves the story revision before approving the plan.
7. `plan-accept-draft` writes the canonical plan and invalidates prior approval as today.

This keeps the current operational model intact while changing only the payload shape.

#### Update / Revision Flow

The revision model becomes much cleaner with slice-first structure.

- **`LOCAL_CORRECTION`**: edit only the targeted `affectedClasses[]` entry or `sharedRules[]` item identified by the risk `planRef`; if the issue is a boundary mismatch, update story.md -> `External Contracts` in the same loop.
- **`SECTION_REWRITE`**: replace the whole target slice object (`S2`) or a shared rule block (`R1`) instead of patching scattered top-level inventories; rewrite story.md -> `External Contracts` only when the external contract understanding changed.
- **`FULL_REPLAN`**: rewrite the full draft as today.

Recommended risk reference strategy:

- Slice-level issues: `S2`
- Class-level issues: `S2-C3`
- Shared-rule issues: `R1`

This is more surgical than the current `M3` / `C8` / `D4` / `VR1` mix because the references now align with how humans think about the work.

External contract issues should stay tied to the owning slice id in `planRef`; the review text can cite story.md -> `External Contracts` when the root cause is a request/response or GitLab contract mismatch.

#### How The Coder Should Consume It

The new coder intake path should be:

1. `summary --feature <name>`
2. Read `currentBatch.slices` directly from summary
3. For each active slice: `plan-get --feature <name> --slice <id>`
4. Read only `readsExisting[]` paths that the slice explicitly names
5. If the slice touches an external boundary, open story.md -> `External Contracts`
6. Implement `affectedClasses[]`
7. Run `batch-verify`

To support this, `plan-get --slice <id>` should return a compact object like:

```json
{
  "feature": "milestones-search-api",
  "revision": 2,
  "scope": { "constraints": ["..."] },
  "sharedRules": [ ...only rules that apply to this slice... ],
  "sharedNotes": [ ...only notes that matter for this slice... ],
  "slice": { ...full slice object... },
  "finalVerification": { "requiredGates": [ ... ], "notes": [ ... ] }
}
```

That means the Coder never needs the whole plan unless explicitly asked.

#### Required `flow-log` Query / Summary Changes

The slice-first plan only pays off if retrieval changes with it.

Required changes:

- Add `plan-get --slice <id>`
- Keep `plan-get --section <name>` for debugging/manual inspection
- Add active slice IDs to `summary`
- Expand `summary.batches` from counts only to something like:

```json
"batches": {
  "current": {
    "batch": 1,
    "slices": ["S1", "S2"]
  },
  "completed": 0,
  "total": 2
}
```

- Add slice metadata to `plan-summary`, at minimum:
  - slice id
  - title
  - dependency ids
  - affected class count
  - estimated test file count

This lets TL and reviewers see whether a slice is too large before implementation starts.

#### Validation Rules Needed For The New Shape

Recommended new validation rules:

- At least one slice is required.
- Each slice must have at least one `affectedClasses[]` entry.
- Each `affectedClasses[]` entry must include `id`, `module`, `package`, `class`, `status`, `purpose`, `change`, and `tests`.
- `tests.levels[]` and `tests.notes` are required for each affected class.
- `readsExisting[]` entries must be non-empty strings.
- `sharedRules[]` entries must include `id`, `rule`, and valid `appliesTo[]` references; `sharedNotes[]` are plain strings.
- Each slice must have `doneWhen[]`.
- A production class locator should appear in **only one slice** by default. If the same class must appear twice, the plan must opt into that explicitly with a short justification. This prevents ambiguous ownership.
- Warn when a slice has more than ~8-10 affected classes or too many expected file touches.
- Plan payloads must not duplicate external request/response examples; those belong in story.md -> `External Contracts`.
- Risk `planRef` and `connectedArea` must validate against slice ids, class ids, and shared-rule ids.

These validations target the real failure mode: slices that are too large or too ambiguous for the Coder.

#### Impact On Each Agent

**Team Lead**

- Must pass active slice ids in the coder handoff, not only feature name.
- Can use slice counts and slice sizes at plan gate to reject overlarge plans early.
- Risk classification becomes clearer because `planRef` can point to a slice or exact class.
- Must re-register and re-approve the story if the Architect updates story.md -> `External Contracts` during planning.

**Product Manager**

- Minimal direct impact.
- Seeds story.md -> `External Contracts` when the feature touches client or GitLab boundaries.
- Indirectly, stories should be kept small enough that they naturally map to one batch unless there is a justified reason not to.

**Java Architect**

- Biggest improvement.
- No separate top-level class/model inventories to maintain.
- Authors one self-contained slice at a time.
- Uses `sharedRules`, `sharedNotes`, and `sliceNotes` without bloating required structural fields.
- Validates and updates story.md -> `External Contracts` from official docs when the feature touches external boundaries.

**Architecture Reviewer**

- Round 1 still reviews the whole plan, but the review unit becomes `sharedRules` + slices, with story.md -> `External Contracts` checked for boundary-heavy slices.
- Risks become more surgical because they can point to `S2` or `S2-C3`.
- The review is easier to keep complete because the plan shape is closer to delivery units.

**Java Coder**

- Should stop reading the entire plan by default.
- Reads `summary`, active slice ids, then one slice payload at a time.
- Does not need to read the full story by default.
- `readsExisting[]` tells Coder exactly which unchanged files matter, reducing broad exploration.
- Reads story.md -> `External Contracts` only when the slice notes or rules indicate an external boundary dependency.

**Code Reviewer**

- Should read the approved story for acceptance criteria, locked constraints, and preserved behavior.
- Can review implementation per active slice and compare changed files directly against `affectedClasses[]`.
- Easier to decide whether the Coder stayed within scope or introduced extra drift.
- Documentation update ownership can stay here without polluting Architect's plan structure.
- Boundary-review checks should compare the code against story.md -> `External Contracts`, not against payloads duplicated in the plan.

**Code Review Refactorer**

- Should consume the same slice payloads as the Coder and Reviewer instead of re-reading the whole plan.
- Finding remediation becomes cleaner because the refactor target can be `S2-C3` rather than a vague whole-plan area.
- This agent's prompt must be aligned with the new slice query surface so it does not conflict with the main review flow.

**Supporting Tooling / Docs Surface**

- `flow-log` docs must describe slice-first retrieval as the default path, not whole-plan retrieval.
- CLI help and prompt instructions must stay aligned on the same reference grammar: `S2`, `S2-C3`, `R1`.
- Example plans in repository docs should be migrated to the new shape quickly, otherwise prompt/doc drift will reappear immediately.

#### Decision On Markdown Alternative

Markdown authoring should **not** be the first move in `v2.5.2`.

It is still a possible fallback if the slice-first JSON shape fails in practice, but it should be treated as **Plan B**.

Why not now:

- It adds a translation layer and a second artifact contract.
- It makes risk link validation, slice retrieval, and coder-targeted consumption harder.
- It solves the wrong problem first. The bigger issue is plan shape, not the existence of JSON itself.

Recommended decision:

- **Primary plan for `v2.5.2`: keep JSON drafts, introduce `schemaVersion: "4.0"`, make slices the primary authoring unit.**
- **Fallback only if needed later:** structured Markdown authoring plus a conversion tool.

#### Implementation Recommendation

Implement this change in two internal steps inside `v2.5.2`:

1. **Schema + query + summary first**
   - New plan shape
   - `plan-get --slice`
   - batch slice ids in `summary`
   - validation updates

2. **Agent prompt rewiring second**
  - PM seeds story.md -> `External Contracts` when applicable
  - Architect validates story.md -> `External Contracts` and writes slice-first plan
   - TL passes active slice ids
   - Coder / Reviewer / Architecture Reviewer default to slice retrieval

This sequencing keeps the tooling contract stable before prompts depend on it.

## Flow Setup

This section captures the **intended v2.5.2 flow after the planned changes land**.

The main planned changes relative to v2.5.1 are:
- slice-first plan authoring and retrieval
- external client and GitLab contracts moved to story.md -> `External Contracts`
- explicit active-slice handoff from TL to Coder
- reduced duplicate TL verification during implementation
- signoff blocked by stale `finalCheck` / `karate`
- documentation removed from Architect plan scope

### Planning Phase Diagram

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ                        INTENDED FLOW                                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   1:TL в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә 2:PM в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә 3:TL в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә 4:ARCH в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә 5:TL      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    create+lock   story.md     register     lean plan.json register   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   5:TL в”Җв”Җв–ә 6:ARCH-REV в—„в”Җв”Җв–ә 4:ARCH (scoped loop) в”Җв”Җв–ә 7:TL           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    increment     risks + planRef    LOCAL/SECTION/FULL   arch-gate в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

### Implementation Phase Diagram

```

### Flow Step Clarification

- **1:TL** вҖ” Requirement lock; `create` + `lock-requirements` with request source path
- **2:PM** вҖ” Produces `artifacts/user-stories/<feature>.story.md`; story must preserve locked scope and acceptance criteria and seed `External Contracts` when the feature touches client or GitLab boundaries
- **3:TL** вҖ” Story gate; `register-artifact story` + `approve-artifact story` as the initial architecture input
- **4:ARCH** вҖ” Validates and updates story.md -> `External Contracts` from official docs when needed, then produces a lean, slice-first `artifacts/implementation-plans/<feature>.plan.json`; capability docs, `.http`, and `context-map.md` are not in scope here
- **5:TL** вҖ” If Architect changed story.md -> `External Contracts`, re-register and re-approve story first; then run `validate-plan` + `plan-summary` + `register-artifact plan` + `approve-artifact plan`; then `increment-round` and invoke Architecture Reviewer
- **6:ARCH-REV** вҖ” Records risks via `add-risk` with required `plan-ref`; TL classifies severity via `reclassify-risk`; TL runs `architecture-gate`
- **7:TL** вҖ” Architecture gate passed; `set-review architectureReview PASS`; starts batch with explicit active slice IDs and hands those slice IDs to Coder
- **8:CODER** вҖ” Reads `summary`, then active slice IDs, then slice-specific plan data; runs `batch-verify` per slice; records files via `add-change`; runs `verify-all` before final handoff
- **9:TL** вҖ” Checks `status` and staleness; re-runs checks only when evidence is stale or suspect; runs final `karate` after final batch if needed
- **10:CODE-REV** вҖ” Records findings via `add-finding`; documentation owner updates `capabilities/<capability>.md`, `.http` examples, and `context-map.md` as needed
- **11:TL** вҖ” Final audit gate; `readiness signoff` must return `ready: true`, and stale `finalCheck` / `karate` are blocking

---

## Runs

Each run is a separate delivery attempt within this version.
Record one expandable section per run. Copy the run template below for each new run.
Between runs, apply fixes and document them in the "Fixes Applied After This Run" subsection.

  - Product Manager: model-name
  - Architect: model-name
  - Coder: model-name
  - Reviewer: model-name

- 
-

- Application started without errors:
- Tests are green:
- Code-quality check passed:
- API is verified:
- Main story functionality is delivered:

## User Suggestions (manually populated by user)

Use this section for improvement ideas for the next version of the flow, tooling, prompts, agent setup, verification, or coding standards.
Applies to the version as a whole, not a single run.

- Keep one story equal to one batch unless there is an explicit reason not to
- Prefer slice-first plans with affected-class entries over very large JSON inventories
- Reduce agent multitasking where possible; documentation and smoke ownership are still candidate splits
- Test Coder on GPT-5.4 or Auto only after the context/handoff fixes land

To be populated after the first `v2.5.2` run using the standard log-template analysis structure.
